title: Crossing Traffic
disclaimer: Training support only. Confirm phraseology, minima, and local procedures with an instructor and current official references.
key_points: Two aircraft on converging tracks at the same flight level will lose separation if neither track or level changes.
The required horizontal separation for two aircraft at the same flight level is 10 NM.
A level change is one valid way to resolve a horizontal conflict before the horizontal minimum is lost.
Separation is maintained once either the horizontal minimum or the vertical minimum is satisfied.

lesson_steps.title: Crossing Traffic
script: Watch AFR612 and RAM401 converge at the same flight level.
Review the required horizontal separation of 10 NM.
Select AFR612 and descend it to FL310.
Watch vertical separation establish as the conflict resolves.

exercise.title: Resolve A Similar Crossing Conflict
exercise.objective: Recognise a developing crossing conflict and resolve it independently, with less guidance than the Learn walkthrough.
student_actions: Choose and issue a resolution before the horizontal minimum is lost.
success_conditions: Vertical separation minimum established before the horizontal minimum would have been lost.
debrief_prompts: Which aircraft did you act on, and why?
Was the resolution issued before the aircraft reached the crossing point?
What was the closest horizontal separation actually reached?
